mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically correlates with economic growth.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, negatively impacting equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Central bank rate hikes typically precede market slowdowns.
failure_condition: If the specified FEDFUNDS trigger is met, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with a low unemployment rate indicate strong consumer demand and economic health, which boosts corporate revenues and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 5.5% and RSAFS 3-month average growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer strength is a key driver of corporate profits.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth is positive and UNRATE is below 5.5%, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts often signals an improving economic outlook and consumer confidence, as housing is a cyclical sector, which translates into broader economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading economic indicator.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows positive 3-month average growth during an early-cycle recovery, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to concerns about central bank tightening and a negative impact on S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can lead to higher interest rates and lower equity valuations.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3.0%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real GDP indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Economic growth is a fundamental driver of stock market performance.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows positive quarterly growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of very low unemployment and rising federal funds rates signals a late-stage economic cycle where the economy may be overheating, prompting aggressive monetary tightening that can precede a market correction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 5.0% and FEDFUNDS has risen by at least 0.10% in the last 3 months
failure_condition: If the UNRATE and FEDFUNDS conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic contraction, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth is negative for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows the specified decline, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.
